data.name: Endocrine Therapy Prior to Randomization Limit
Physical Exam Not Repeated if Within 7 Days of Randomization
Study Treatment Delay for Logistical Reasons
Unscheduled Visit Required if >40 Days Between V26 and V27
No Blood Donation for 3 Months After Discontinuation
Randomization Within 16 Months of Surgery
data.description: Patient must be randomized within 16 months of definitive breast cancer surgery
data.text: The patient must be randomized within 16 months from the time of definitive breast cancer surgery.

data.name: Endocrine Therapy Duration Prior to Randomization
data.description: Up to 12 weeks of endocrine therapy allowed prior to randomization
data.text: Patients may have up to 12 weeks of endocrine therapy following their last non-endocrine therapy (surgery, chemotherapy, or radiotherapy) prior to randomization.

data.name: ECOG Performance Status
data.description: Patient must have ECOG PS ≤1
data.text: The patient has a performance status ≤1 on the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group scale.

data.name: Adequate Organ Function
data.description: Patient must have adequate organ function as defined by protocol criteria
data.text: The patient has adequate organ function for all of the following criteria.

data.name: Recovery from Chemotherapy
data.description: Patient must have recovered from acute effects of chemotherapy to CTCAE Grade ≤1
data.text: Patients must have recovered (CTCAE Grade ≤1) from the acute effects of chemotherapy except for residual alopecia or Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy prior to randomization.

data.name: Recovery from Radiotherapy
data.description: Patient must have recovered from acute effects of radiotherapy to Grade ≤1
data.text: Patients must have recovered (Grade ≤1) from the acute effects of radiotherapy.

data.name: Negative Pregnancy Test
data.description: Women of reproductive potential must have negative blood pregnancy test within 14 days prior to randomization
data.text: Women of reproductive potential must have a negative blood pregnancy test at baseline (within 14 days prior to randomization).

data.name: ALT/AST Grade 4 Discontinuation
data.description: ALT/AST >20x ULN requires abemaciclib discontinuation
data.text: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN): Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Hy's Law Discontinuation
data.description: Elevation in AST/ALT >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN in absence of cholestasis requires discontinuation
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3 x ULN with total bilirubin >2 x ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4 Discontinuation
data.description: Grade 3 or 4 ILD/pneumonitis requires abemaciclib discontinuation
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Significant Noncompliance
data.description: Patient repeatedly misses >25% of abemaciclib doses or takes ≥125% of prescribed amount
data.text: A patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses between scheduled clinic visits. Similarly, a patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he is judged by the investigator to have intentionally or repeatedly taken more than (≥125%) the prescribed amount of medication.

data.name: IDFS Event per STEEP Criteria
data.description: Occurrence of invasive disease-free survival event per STEEP criteria
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria.

data.name: Lowest Dose Requiring Further Reduction
data.description: Patient on 50 mg twice daily requiring further dose reduction must discontinue abemaciclib
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50-mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3 Dose Suspension
data.description: Grade 3 hematologic toxicity requires dose suspension until resolution to at least Grade 2
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2.

data.name: Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 Hematologic Toxicity
data.description: Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 hematologic toxicity requires dose suspension and reduction
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Diarrhea Grade 2 Not Resolving Within 24 Hours
data.description: Grade 2 diarrhea not resolving within 24 hours requires dose suspension
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1.

data.name: Non-hematologic Toxicity Persistent/Recurrent Grade 2 or Grade 3-4
data.description: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 not resolving within 7 days, or Grade 3-4 requires suspension and reduction
data.text: Non-hematologic Toxicity: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures within 7 days to baseline or Grade 1, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to either baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Suspension >28 Days Requires Discussion
data.description: Dose suspensions of 28 days or more must be discussed with Lilly CRP/CRS
data.text: Dose suspensions ≥28 days must be discussed with Lilly CRP/CRS.

data.name: Screening to On-Study Treatment Transition
data.text: Patient must meet all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria. First dose of study treatment must be taken no later than 3 days after randomization. Eligibility must be based on the last available screening laboratory results prior to randomization.

data.name: On-Study Treatment to Short-Term Follow-Up Transition
data.text: Short-term follow-up visit takes place 30 days (±5 days) after completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period or after discontinuation criteria are met and decision is made for the patient to discontinue all study treatment, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Short-Term Follow-Up to Long-Term Follow-Up Transition
data.text: The long-term follow-up period begins the day after the short-term follow-up visit. ALL patients will enter the long-term follow-up period.

data.name: Discontinuation for ALT/AST Grade 4
data.text: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN): Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Hy's Law Criteria
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3 x ULN with total bilirubin >2 x ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Lowest Dose Requiring Further Reduction
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50-mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Discontinuation for IDFS Event
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria. Study treatment must be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Significant Noncompliance
data.text: Patients who are significantly noncompliant with abemaciclib may be discontinued from abemaciclib treatment. A patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses between scheduled clinic visits.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: The patient becomes pregnant during the study.

data.name: Discontinuation for Unacceptable Toxicity
data.text: Unacceptable toxicity as determined by investigator.

data.name: Discontinuation for Enrollment in Another Trial
data.text: The patient is enrolled in any other clinical trial involving an investigational product or any other type of medical research judged not to be scientifically or medically compatible with this study.

data.name: Abemaciclib Dose Reduction Level 1
data.text: Abemaciclib reduced from 150 mg twice daily to 100 mg twice daily for qualifying toxicity events as per dose adjustment table.

data.name: Abemaciclib Dose Reduction Level 2
data.text: Abemaciclib reduced from 100 mg twice daily to 50 mg twice daily for qualifying toxicity events as per dose adjustment table.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3 Dose Suspension
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 Dose Modification
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Growth Factor Use Dose Modification
data.text: If patient requires administration of blood cell growth factors: Dose MUST be suspended for at least 48 hours after the last dose of growth factors and until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level unless already performed.

data.name: Diarrhea Grade 2 Dose Suspension
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Diarrhea Persistent/Recurrent Grade 2 or Grade 3-4 Dose Modification
data.text: Diarrhea persistent or recurrent Grade 2 that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures, or requires hospitalization, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: ALT/AST Grade 2-3 Dose Modification
data.text: ALT/AST Increased persistent or recurrent Grade 2 (>3.0-5.0×ULN), or Grade 3 (>5.0-20.0×ULN): Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 2 Dose Modification
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 2: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Arm A Single Drug Discontinuation Continuation Rule
data.text: For Arm A only, if a patient discontinues only one of the combination drugs (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) prior to completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period for a reason other than an IDFS event per STEEP criteria, she/he should continue the other drug until completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period or other discontinuation criteria are met, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Endocrine Therapy Switch
data.text: A switch to another standard endocrine therapy is allowed as per the investigator's discretion and in the absence of an IDFS event per STEEP criteria, during the on-study treatment period.

data.name: Early Termination - Investigator Decision
data.description: The investigator decides that the patient should be discontinued from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Patient Decision
data.description: The patient requests to be withdrawn from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Sponsor Decision
data.description: Lilly stops the study or stops the patient's participation in the study for medical, safety, regulatory, or other reasons consistent with applicable laws, regulations, and GCP.

data.name: Early Termination - Pregnancy
data.description: The patient becomes pregnant during the study.

data.name: Early Termination - Significant Noncompliance
data.description: The patient is significantly noncompliant with study procedures and/or treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - IDFS Event
data.description: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria.

data.name: Early Termination - Unacceptable Toxicity
data.description: The patient experiences unacceptable toxicity requiring discontinuation of all study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - ALT/AST Grade 4
data.description: ALT/AST >20.0×ULN (Grade 4) requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination - Hy's Law
data.description: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN in the absence of cholestasis requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination - ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4
data.description: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4 requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination - Enrollment in Incompatible Trial
data.description: The patient is enrolled in any other clinical trial involving an investigational product or medical research judged not to be scientifically or medically compatible with this study.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: Completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period followed by short-term follow-up visit and long-term follow-up through Year 10.

data.name: Early Termination - Abemaciclib Lowest Dose Further Reduction Required
data.description: If a patient receiving the 50-mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Visit 27 End of Treatment Decision
data.description: Decision point at 24 months (Visit 27) anchored to Visit 1 to determine completion of 2-year on-study treatment period and transition to short-term follow-up.

data.name: Dose Modification Decision at Each Clinic Visit
data.description: At each clinic visit, assess toxicity and determine if dose suspension, dose reduction, or discontinuation of abemaciclib is required per dose adjustment table.

data.name: Unscheduled Visit Decision Between V26 and V27
data.description: If there are >40 days between Visit 26 and Visit 27, unscheduled visits must be scheduled to complete the 2-year period.

data.name: Single Drug Discontinuation Decision (Arm A)
data.description: For Arm A, if a patient discontinues only one drug (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) for a reason other than an IDFS event, the other drug should continue until completion of 2-year treatment period or other discontinuation criteria are met.

data.name: Dose Suspension >28 Days Decision
data.description: For patients not recovering from toxicity within 28 days, a delay >28 days is permitted upon agreement between the investigator and the Lilly CRP/CRS and abemaciclib dose adjustment is to be considered.

data.name: Rescreening Eligibility Decision
data.description: A patient who fails screening is allowed to screen again after signing a new ICF under specified conditions: protocol amendment eligibility change, patient status change, or extenuating circumstances preventing enrollment.
